steps.task_description: Analyse requirements for `slugify(text: str) -> str`. List the character classes that must be preserved (lowercase ASCII letters and digits), the separators that must collapse to a single dash, the unicode handling expected (NFKD-decompose and strip combining marks), the leading/trailing dash trimming rule, and the empty-input return value. Produce a numbered list of 6–8 acceptance criteria suitable for direct use as test-case generators.

Implement `slugify(text: str) -> str` in pure Python (stdlib only — unicodedata + re).  Output must satisfy every acceptance criterion produced by the `analyse` step.  Do not import third-party packages.  Wrap the function in a single self-contained module-level definition with a one-line docstring; no example usage.

Write a pytest module `test_slugify.py` covering every acceptance criterion from the `analyse` step.  Each test name should map to its criterion number (e.g. `test_ac3_collapses_separators`).  Cover one happy path, one Unicode case, one empty string, and one all-separators edge case at minimum.

Review the `implement` output against the `test` output.  Identify (a) any acceptance criterion the implementation misses, (b) any pytest case that would fail given the implementation, (c) any code-quality concerns (dead branches, unused imports, exception silencing).  Produce a structured review with `gaps`, `bugs`, and `quality` sections.
